dDreggNet live network
light client · idle
network drive ›
recursive-STARK light client · in this tab

Don’t trust the server.
Verify it yourself.

Every sovereign cell on DreggNet carries a proof that its entire committed history was authorized, conservative, and correctly committed. A light client checks that proof here, in your browser — re-running nothing, trusting no server.

See the living network ↓
dregg_wasm · recursive-STARK verifier
●
Proof engine idle
a verifier, not a viewer — press run
the living network

Sovereign cells, proving themselves

The custodial hub and its cells, read live from the edge node. When the light client runs, the verification sweeps the network — each cell lights up as it’s checked against the cryptographic floor.

network idle
connecting to the edge…
The light client runs in your tab. Open any cell to watch its committed history fold and verify against the cryptographic floor — per-field heap openings included.
what you’re looking at

A turn, a token, a receipt

dregg has four words. A cell is the unit of state and identity. A capability is an attenuable, proof-carrying token of authority. A turn is one authorized step over owned state. A receipt is its verifiable witness.

01 — the token

Authority is production, not possession

You hold a capability iff you can produce a witness the kernel accepts — never merely be named in a table. Sharing is attenuation: a strictly weaker token, provably so.

02 — the turn

Every step is a verifiable fact

A turn is a forest of cap-gated actions, committed all-or-nothing. Tampering a field the effect didn’t legitimately write makes the turn unprovable — the anti-ghost property.

03 — the proof

One root knows the whole history

Each turn carries a STARK; the recursion folds them into one root. A light client holding that root learns every transition was genuine — re-executing nothing.

dDreggNet

The read-only trustless network view plus the interactive drive layer. The verification you see runs entirely client-side; the server is never trusted, only checked.

Drive the network › — connect an identity and fire real cap-gated turns: publish a minisite, open a metered lease, fire a transfer. Then verify the resulting cell trustlessly, in-tab.
